services.meta_title: Entry Door Installation Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Expert entry door installation across Metro Detroit. Fiberglass, steel & wood front door replacement built for Michigan winters. Get a free estimate today.
services.h1: Entry Door Installation & Front Door Replacement in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: Your front door is the first thing guests see and your last line of defense against a Michigan winter—let's make it count.
services.intro_lead: A drafty, sticking, or storm-battered front door quietly drags down your home's comfort, security, and curb appeal every single day. BH Door Solutions handles entry door installation and front door replacement across Metro Detroit—doors chosen right, hung plumb, and sealed tight so they hold up through every freeze-thaw. You get honest flat-rate pricing, a free estimate, and one clean visit.

sections.h2: When It's Time for Front Door Replacement
body: Michigan doors take a beating. If yours sticks in the summer humidity and won't latch, lets in a draft you can feel, shows rot or rust along the bottom rail, or rattles loose in its frame, replacement usually beats another patch. A door that no longer seals is running up your heating bill and leaving your home easier to force open.

We also tackle the urgent stuff promptly during business hours—doors damaged in a break-in attempt, warped by a storm, or that suddenly won't lock or close—so your home is buttoned up again the same day when the schedule allows.
bullets: Drafts you can feel, or a door that whistles in the wind
Sticking, dragging, or a latch that no longer lines up
Rot, rust, or water damage along the frame or bottom rail
Cracked, foggy, or single-pane glass in the door or sidelights
A dated door dragging down your curb appeal and home value

sections.h2: Choosing Your Entry Door: Fiberglass, Steel & Wood
body: The right material comes down to your budget, how exposed the entry is, and the look you want. We install all three main types and walk you through them honestly—no upselling you into more door than your entryway needs.
bullets: Fiberglass entry door: our most popular pick—it won't rot, warp, dent, or rust, mimics real wood grain, and its insulated core stands up beautifully to Michigan freeze-thaw.
Steel entry door: the toughest, most budget-friendly choice for security, with a foam-insulated core that blocks the cold—ideal for high-traffic and light commercial entrances.
Solid wood door: timeless, substantial, and fully customizable for character homes—best under a covered porch where it's shielded from direct weather.

body: Bigger openings make a statement—and they demand precise installation to stay square, sealed, and smooth-swinging for years. We handle grand entrances from measurement to final adjustment.
bullets: Double / double-entry doors: matched pairs with an active and stationary leaf, hung dead-level so both panels meet and seal.
Sidelights: slim glass panels flanking the door that pull daylight into your foyer without giving up insulation.
Transoms: the window above the door that adds height and light—flashed and sealed to keep Michigan rain and snow out.

body: A great door is only as good as its install, and a proper entry door installation starts long before the new door goes in. The problems we most often fix are doors that were never shimmed plumb, frames left un-insulated, and skipped flashing—all of which invite drafts, leaks, and a door that binds the moment the seasons turn.

We remove the old unit, inspect and repair the rough opening, set the new door plumb and square, insulate and flash it properly, then finish with weatherstripping and a threshold that actually seals. Our trucks arrive fully stocked, so most residential and light commercial installs are done in a single visit.

whats_included: Free in-home measurement and a no-obligation written estimate
Removal and haul-away of your old door and debris
Your new entry door—fiberglass, steel, or solid wood, slab or pre-hung
Inspection and repair of the rough opening before the new door goes in
Professional plumb-and-square setting with proper shimming and insulation
Weatherstripping, threshold, sweep, and exterior caulk or flashing for a tight seal
New deadbolt and handleset installation and alignment
Final walkthrough and a workmanship guarantee on the finished job

why_us.title: Michigan Homes Are What We Know
why_us.text: We install entry doors for our Metro Detroit neighbors week in and week out, so we know exactly how local freeze-thaw swings, summer humidity, and wind-driven rain punish an entryway—and we spec and seal every door to handle it.

why_us.title: One Visit, Fully Stocked Trucks
why_us.text: Our trucks carry the doors, hardware, shims, flashing, and sealants a proper install needs, so most entry door replacements are finished in a single visit—no half-done openings and no waiting around for a second trip.

why_us.title: Honest, Flat-Rate Pricing
why_us.text: You get a free, no-obligation estimate and a flat, upfront price before we start—no surprise add-ons and no pressure. We're licensed and insured, and a workmanship guarantee stands behind every door we hang.

faqs.q: How long does entry door installation take?
faqs.a: Most standard entry door installations are finished in a single day, often just a few hours. A straightforward pre-hung swap moves quickly, while custom sizes, double doors, or added sidelights and transoms take longer. Because our trucks arrive fully stocked with doors, hardware, and sealants, we complete the large majority of jobs in one visit.

faqs.q: Fiberglass or steel—which is better for a Michigan entry door?
faqs.a: Fiberglass is the best all-around choice for most Metro Detroit homes: it resists rot, warping, and rust, insulates well, and shrugs off freeze-thaw without denting. A steel entry door costs less and offers excellent security, making it great for budget-focused or light commercial entrances, though it can dent. We'll help you decide during your free estimate.

faqs.q: How much does a new front door cost?
faqs.a: Your exact price depends on the door material, size, glass, hardware, and any framing repairs your opening needs. As a typical range, an installed entry door often runs from a few hundred dollars for a basic steel unit to several thousand for premium fiberglass or custom wood with sidelights. Get a free estimate for a firm, flat-rate number.

faqs.q: Do you replace just the door or the whole frame?
faqs.a: Both—we do slab-only replacements when your existing frame is solid, and full pre-hung installs when the frame is rotted, out of square, or damaged. During your free estimate we inspect the rough opening and jambs, then recommend the option that will seal properly and last. If we find hidden rot, we repair it before setting your new door.

services.cta_line: Ready for a front door that seals tight and turns heads? Call BH Door Solutions at (313) 236-4558 for your free, no-obligation entry door installation estimate.

services.h1: Patio Door Installation for Metro Detroit Homes & Businesses
services.hero_tagline: Sliding glass, French, and folding wall systems—expertly installed to keep Michigan weather where it belongs: outside.
services.intro_lead: A patio door is the biggest moving piece of glass in your home, and when it sticks, drafts, or won't lock, you feel it every single day. Whether your old slider has fogged between the panes, jumped its track, or leaks cold air all winter, professional patio door installation fixes it for good. BH Door Solutions installs and replaces sliding, French, and folding glass doors for homes and businesses across metro Detroit.

body: Sliding glass doors are the workhorse of the Michigan home—perfect for decks, patios, and tight spaces where a swinging door won't fit. Our sliding patio door installation covers everything from single-panel replacements to wide multi-panel units, all set on smooth rollers that won't bind or grind. We swap out worn tracks, upgrade to insulated Low-E glass, and seal the frame tight against drafts.
bullets: Energy-efficient Low-E, argon-filled double-pane glass
Heavy-duty rollers and reinforced tracks that glide instead of stick
Foot-locks and anti-lift hardware for real security

body: When you want the classic swing-open look and full-width views, French doors deliver. Our french door installation includes both in-swing and out-swing configurations, single and double units, with the option of a fixed panel or a true double-active pair. We hang them plumb and true so both leaves latch cleanly and the astragal seals flush—no gaps, no rattle, no daylight showing through the seam.
bullets: In-swing or out-swing, single or double-door layouts
Multi-point locking hardware for a tight, secure seal
Adjustable sills and full weatherstripping for a draft-free close

body: Want to open an entire wall to the backyard or a restaurant patio? Multi-slide and bi-fold (accordion) glass wall systems stack or pocket away to erase the line between inside and out. These are precision systems—the panels have to be perfectly aligned and the sill drainage has to be right, or they bind and leak. We handle the structural prep, threshold detailing, and panel tuning for both homes and commercial spaces.
bullets: Multi-slide, pocketing, and bi-fold folding configurations
Low-profile, ADA-compliant sill options for commercial jobs
Structural opening prep and precise panel alignment

body: Metro Detroit's freeze-thaw cycle is brutal on patio doors. Water works into a worn track or a failed seal, freezes overnight, and pries the door out of alignment until it won't slide or lock. We install units rated for our climate—thermally broken frames, quality weatherstripping, and proper sill drainage—so your door stays tight through January and cool through July.
If your current door is drafty, foggy between the panes, or frozen to its track, those are all signs the seal has already failed and it's time to replace it.

sections.h2: What to Expect: Our Installation Process
body: Every patio door installation starts with a free, no-obligation in-home estimate, where we measure the opening, check the framing, and give you honest flat-rate pricing up front. On install day, our fully-stocked truck carries the door, hardware, flashing, and sealant we need to finish in one visit.

We remove the old unit, inspect the opening for hidden water or rot damage, set the new door level and square, insulate and flash the frame, then test every lock, latch, and roller before we clean up and go.

whats_included: Free in-home measurement and a no-obligation written estimate
Removal and haul-away of your old door and debris
Inspection of the rough opening for hidden water damage or rot
Professional leveling, shimming, and squaring of the new unit
Insulation, flashing, and weathertight sealing around the frame
New locks, handles, rollers, and weatherstripping installed
Interior and exterior trim finishing for a clean, finished look
A full function test of every lock, latch, and glide before we leave

why_us.title: One-Visit Installs, Fully-Stocked Trucks
why_us.text: We arrive with the door, hardware, flashing, and sealant already on the truck, so most patio door installations are finished start-to-finish in a single visit—no waiting around for a second trip.

why_us.title: Michigan Homes Are Our Specialty
why_us.text: We're local metro Detroit installers who know how freeze-thaw, storm damage, and winter drafts attack a patio door—and we set units and seals built to stand up to it, season after season.

why_us.title: Honest, Flat-Rate Pricing
why_us.text: You get an upfront, flat-rate quote at your free estimate—no hourly surprises, no pressure. Sliding, French, or a full folding glass wall, you'll know the price before we start the job.

faqs.q: How long does patio door installation take?
faqs.a: Most standard sliding or French patio door installations take a single day, often just a few hours. A straightforward panel replacement goes fast, while a wider multi-slide or folding glass wall system, or an opening that needs framing repair, can take longer. We give you a realistic timeline at your free in-home estimate so there are no surprises on install day.

faqs.q: Should I repair or replace my sliding door?
faqs.a: Replace it if the glass is fogged between the panes, the frame is rotted, or it ices off its track every winter—those point to a failed seal or frame. If the door just sticks or a roller is worn, a repair may be enough. We'll tell you honestly which makes sense at your free estimate, never pushing a replacement you don't need.

faqs.q: Can you install a French door where my old slider is?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases we can replace a sliding door with French patio doors, or the other way around. The opening width usually works for either style, though we'll check the framing and header first. Swapping styles is a popular upgrade for better curb appeal and airflow. We'll confirm what fits your opening and give you flat-rate pricing at your free estimate.

faqs.q: Do you install patio doors for businesses too?
faqs.a: Yes, we install and replace patio and folding glass wall systems for commercial spaces as well as homes—restaurants, offices, storefronts, and patios. We offer low-profile, ADA-compliant sill options and can schedule around your operations during the business day. One call covers both your residential and commercial door needs across Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties.

services.cta_line: Ready for a patio door that glides, seals, and locks like new? Call BH Door Solutions at (313) 236-4558 for your free, no-obligation estimate anywhere in metro Detroit.

services.meta_title: Storm Door Installation Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Storm door installation across Metro Detroit. New storm, screen & security doors built for Michigan weather. Free estimates & same-day service. Call today.
services.h1: Storm Door Installation & Screen Doors in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: New storm, screen, and security doors that stand up to Michigan winters, installed in one visit.
services.intro_lead: When a Michigan winter sends drafts whistling past your front door, or a summer evening forces you to choose between fresh air and a house full of bugs, the right storm door solves both. BH Door Solutions handles storm door installation for homes across Metro Detroit, matching the door to your home, your climate, and the way you actually live. It's a small upgrade that quietly pays you back every season.

sections.h2: Storm Door Installation Built for Michigan Weather
body: A quality storm door is the first line of defense between your home and everything Michigan throws at it: wind-driven rain, winter drafts, road salt, and blazing July sun. Our storm door installation adds an insulating buffer in front of your entry door, cutting energy loss in the cold months and letting fresh air flow in the warm ones.

We install full-view, high-view, and ventilating models in aluminum and steel, color-matched to your home. If your current door sags, won't latch, or has a torn screen and a worn-out closer, a storm door replacement restores the tight seal and the curb appeal in a single afternoon.
bullets: Full-view glass, high-view, and ventilating storm door styles
Aluminum and steel frames color-matched to your entry
Storm door replacement for sagging, drafty, or damaged doors

body: Screen door installation keeps the bugs out and the breeze in once Michigan finally warms up. Combination doors take it a step further with interchangeable glass and screen panels, so one door works all four seasons, glass in for winter insulation and screen in for summer airflow.
We set the panels, tune the pneumatic closer, and make sure every swap slides smoothly so you're never wrestling with a stuck insert on the first warm day of the year.

sections.h2: Retractable Screens That Disappear When You Don't Need Them
body: Prefer a screen that vanishes when it's not in use? Retractable screens roll neatly into a slim housing along the door frame, giving you unobstructed glass and clear views in winter, then pull across on demand for ventilation in summer.
They're a clean, low-profile upgrade for front entries and patio doors where a traditional swinging screen would just get in the way or take a beating from the wind.

sections.h2: Security Storm Doors for Peace of Mind
body: Security storm doors combine the weather protection of a standard storm door with a heavy-gauge steel or aluminum frame, tamper-resistant hinges, and a deadbolt-ready lock. They let you open your main door for light and air while staying locked and screened.
It's a smart upgrade for side and rear doors that face the alley or backyard, and for any entry where you want a visible, solid layer of added protection.

Every storm door installation starts with a free, no-obligation estimate and precise measurements, so there's no guesswork and no surprise fees. We handle out-of-square and older frames that big-box installers don't want to touch.

On install day, our fully stocked truck means we remove your old door, mount and square the new frame, hang the door, fit the weatherstripping and sweep, and set the hardware in one visit. Before we leave, we test the swing, latch, and seal with you and haul the old door away.

whats_included: Precise measurement of your existing door opening and frame
Removal and haul-away of your old storm or screen door
New storm, screen, combination, or security door in your chosen style and color
Interchangeable glass and screen inserts fitted and tested
Weatherstripping and a tight door sweep to seal out drafts
Pneumatic closer and safety chain adjusted for smooth, controlled closing
Secure hardware install: handle, deadbolt-ready latch, and hinges
Final cleanup and a walkthrough to check the swing, latch, and seal

why_us.title: Built for Michigan's Freeze-Thaw
why_us.text: We install and seal doors for a climate that swings from sub-zero mornings to humid summers. Proper weatherstripping, the right closer, and a tight sweep mean your door still shuts clean after the first hard freeze and doesn't stick when the humidity climbs.

why_us.title: One Visit, Fully Stocked Trucks
why_us.text: Our trucks carry the doors, hardware, and weatherproofing to finish most installs the same day. No second trip and no waiting a week for parts: we measure, install, and clean up in one appointment when the schedule allows.

why_us.title: Local Specialists, Honest Flat Rates
why_us.text: We're metro Detroit door specialists, not a national franchise. You get a free estimate, upfront flat-rate pricing with no surprise fees, and a workmanship guarantee standing behind every door we hang.

faqs.q: How long does storm door installation take?
faqs.a: Most storm door installations take two to four hours for a single door. Our trucks arrive fully stocked, so we measure, remove your old door, mount and square the new frame, hang the door, and seal everything in one visit. Larger openings or custom security doors can run a bit longer, but same-day completion during business hours is the norm.

faqs.q: Storm door vs. screen door: which one do I need?
faqs.a: A storm door has interchangeable glass and screen panels, so it protects against winter drafts and summer bugs year-round; a screen door only handles airflow and insects. In Michigan's freeze-thaw climate, most homeowners choose a combination storm door for the extra insulation and weather protection. We'll help you pick the right fit during your free estimate.

faqs.q: Can you install a storm door on any entry door?
faqs.a: Almost always, yes. We install storm, screen, and security doors on standard entry, side, and rear doors, and we handle out-of-square or older frames that big-box installers won't touch. If your opening needs a custom size or extra reinforcement, we measure precisely and order the right door as part of your free, no-obligation estimate.

faqs.q: Will a storm door actually cut down on drafts?
faqs.a: Yes. A properly sealed storm door adds an insulating air pocket in front of your entry door, which cuts winter drafts and helps hold heat during Michigan cold snaps. In summer, switching to the screen insert pulls a breeze through without letting bugs in. Correct weatherstripping and a tight door sweep are what make the difference.

services.cta_line: Ready for a door that keeps drafts out and curb appeal up? Call (313) 236-4558 for your free, no-obligation storm door installation estimate.

services.meta_title: Interior Door Installation Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Interior door installation across Metro Detroit — prehung, slab, barn & French doors fit right the first time. Free estimates & same-day service. Call today.
services.h1: Interior Door Installation in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: New doors that swing true, latch clean, and match every room in your Michigan home.
services.intro_lead: A door that sticks in summer, won't latch in winter, or scrapes the frame every time you open it usually isn't the door's fault — it's the install. BH Door Solutions handles interior door installation across Metro Detroit, setting every door plumb, level, and square so it works the day we leave and years after. From a single bedroom slab to a full house of prehung doors, it's one crew and one visit.

body: Every interior door installation starts with picking the right type for the opening. A prehung door arrives already mounted in its own frame with the hinges attached — the cleanest option when the old jamb is damaged, out of square, or you're changing the door size. A slab is just the door itself, which we hang on your existing hinges and frame when the jamb is still solid. Prehung door installation takes longer but gives you a fresh, square opening; slab swaps are faster and easier on the budget.

Michigan's humidity swings are hard on interior doors. Wood absorbs summer moisture and swells, then shrinks in the dry winter heat — which is why a door that closed fine in July binds by January. We set proper reveal gaps, shim the jambs true, and hang the hinges so your door keeps swinging clean through every freeze-thaw season.
bullets: Prehung installs when the frame is damaged, out of square, or being resized
Slab swaps when your existing jamb is solid and true
Correct reveal gaps set for Michigan's seasonal swell and shrink

body: Closet and bifold doors take more abuse than almost any door in the house — kids yank them, tracks bend, and cheap rollers jump the rail. We install bifold, sliding bypass, and hinged closet doors on tracks that actually stay aligned, with the pivots and guides adjusted so panels fold flat and glide without catching. Whether you're finishing a bedroom, a hall closet, or the laundry, we make sure the doors clear the carpet, the trim, and each other.

Sliding barn doors are the upgrade everyone wants — but done wrong, they wobble, scrape the wall, or don't fully cover the opening. Barn door installation is really about the hardware and the backing: we locate the studs or add solid blocking behind the drywall so the track carries the door's full weight without sagging. Then we hang the door with the right gap, add a floor guide to stop the sway, and set soft-close stops so it lands quiet.

The result is the farmhouse look that holds up, not a decorative piece that rattles every time you slide it. We handle single doors and double-door openings, on wood or on rooms where you want the extra light of a glass panel.

Pocket doors are the space-saver for tight bathrooms, closets, and hallways where a swinging door would waste floor space. We install new pocket-door kits and repair existing ones — replacing bent tracks, worn rollers, and split guides so the door slides fully into the wall and back out without jumping the rail. When a pocket door disappears crooked or won't stay open, it's almost always a track or roller problem, and our stocked trucks carry the parts to fix it in one visit during business hours.

Interior French doors bring light and a wider, open feel between rooms — living room to office, or a primary bedroom to a sitting area. The trick with a French pair is alignment: both doors have to meet evenly in the middle, latch clean, and swing without one dragging. We set the jamb dead plumb, adjust the hinges, and dial in the astragal and strike so the pair closes flush every time.
Solid or glass-panel, we hang them so the center seam stays tight and both doors work as one — a finished interior door installation you'll notice every time you walk through.

whats_included: Free in-home measure and a no-obligation, flat-rate estimate
Removal and haul-away of your old door and hardware
New door hung plumb, level, and square with correct reveal gaps
Hinges, handlesets, and latch hardware installed and adjusted
Jamb shimming, with trim and casing reset or replaced as needed
Barn and pocket door track, rollers, floor guides, and wall blocking
Final adjustment so the door latches clean and swings without binding
Full cleanup of the work area before we leave

why_us.title: Fully-stocked trucks, one-visit installs
why_us.text: We roll up with shims, hinges, tracks, rollers, and common door sizes already on board — so prehung, slab, barn, and pocket doors get done in a single visit instead of a return trip for parts.

why_us.title: We know how Michigan homes move
why_us.text: Freeze-thaw and humidity swings make doors swell, stick, and drop out of square. We set your doors with the right reveals and shimming so they keep working through every Metro Detroit season, not just on installation day.

why_us.title: Honest flat-rate pricing, free estimates
why_us.text: You get an upfront, flat-rate quote before we start — no hourly surprises. Every interior door installation comes with a free, no-obligation estimate and our workmanship guarantee.

faqs.q: What's the difference between a prehung door and a slab door?
faqs.a: A prehung door comes already mounted in a complete frame with the hinges attached, while a slab is just the door panel you hang on an existing frame. Prehung is best when your jamb is damaged, out of square, or you're resizing the opening. Slab installs are faster and cost less when the existing frame is still solid and square.

faqs.q: How long does interior door installation take?
faqs.a: Most single interior door installations take one to two hours, including removing the old door, hanging the new one, and adjusting the hardware. A slab swap on a good frame is quicker; a prehung door, barn door, or a whole house of doors takes longer. When our schedule allows, we can often finish same-day installs during business hours.

faqs.q: Can you install a sliding barn door on any wall?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes, but the wall needs solid backing to carry the door's weight. During barn door installation we locate the studs or add blocking behind the drywall so the track won't sag or pull loose over time. We also confirm the wall beside the opening is wide enough for the door to slide fully clear.

faqs.q: Do you repair pocket doors, or only install new ones?
faqs.a: We do both. We install new pocket-door kits and repair existing ones that stick, jump the track, or won't stay open — usually a bent track, worn rollers, or a broken guide. Because our trucks are stocked with common pocket-door parts, we can often diagnose and fix the problem in a single visit during business hours.

services.cta_line: Ready for doors that fit right and swing true? Call BH Door Solutions at (313) 236-4558 for your free interior door installation estimate anywhere in Metro Detroit.

services.meta_title: Door Repair Near Me | Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Searching door repair near me in Metro Detroit? BH fixes sticking, drafty, won't-latch doors, often same-day. Free estimates, flat-rate pricing. Call today.
services.h1: Door Repair Near Me in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: Sticking, drafty, or won't latch? We fix doors right the first time, with same-day service available across Metro Detroit.
services.intro_lead: A door that sticks, drags, or won't latch isn't just annoying, it's a security gap and an energy drain, especially through a Michigan winter. If you've been searching door repair near me, BH Door Solutions gets to homes and businesses across Metro Detroit fast, often the same day. We find the real cause and fix it in one visit, so the problem doesn't come back next season.

body: When a door suddenly rubs the frame or drags on the floor, the cause is almost always seasonal movement. Michigan's freeze-thaw cycle swells wood doors in humid months and shifts frames as the ground heaves and settles, so a door that fit fine in fall binds up by spring.

We find the true cause before we ever reach for a plane. Sometimes it's a loose hinge screw pulling the door out of square, sometimes the slab has swollen and needs a light trim and reseal, and sometimes the frame itself has moved. A quick door repair near me search often ends here, because we fix the source instead of shimming a symptom that returns in a month.

sections.h2: Door Alignment & Adjustment (Won't Close or Latch)
body: A door that won't close or latch almost always comes down to alignment. When the latch no longer lines up with the strike plate, the door pops open on its own, rattles in the wind, or needs a hard shove to catch.

Our door alignment & adjustment work resets the door square in its opening. We tighten or shim the hinges, reposition the strike plate, and even out the gap (the reveal) around all four edges. On exterior doors we also confirm the deadbolt throws cleanly, because a misaligned lock is a lock that fails right when you need it.
bullets: Door pops open or won't stay shut
Latch or deadbolt won't catch without lifting the door
Uneven gaps around the frame
Rattling or wind whistling through the edges

body: Hinges carry the full weight of the door every time it swings, so they're often the first thing to fail. Stripped screw holes, bent knuckles, and worn pins lead to sagging, popping, and that grinding squeal that always gets louder in the cold.

Our hinge repair ranges from re-anchoring stripped screws with longer fasteners that bite into the framing stud, to swapping bent or corroded hinges for matching hardware. For heavy steel and high-traffic commercial doors, we install ball-bearing hinges built for constant use, so a busy entrance keeps swinging smoothly. When people look up door repair near me for a sagging front door, this is usually the fix.

If you can feel a cold draft or see daylight around a closed door, you're heating the outdoors. Worn weatherstripping, a gap under the slab, and a warped threshold let a Michigan winter pour straight into your entryway and push your energy bills up.

We reseal the opening with fresh weatherstripping, adjust or replace the door sweep and threshold, and correct the gaps or warping that break the seal. The result is a tight, quiet door that keeps the heat in and the drafts, water, and street noise out.

Kicked-in panels, rot along the bottom rail, dents in a steel door, cracked glass inserts, and storm damage all weaken a door's security and its curb appeal. In many cases the whole door doesn't need replacing, only the damaged component does.

We handle these urgent repairs promptly, and same-day when the schedule allows during business hours. We repair or replace panels, rails, glass inserts, and hardware, and we'll tell you honestly when a repair makes sense versus when replacement is the smarter spend. Either way, you get a straight answer and an upfront flat-rate price before any work begins.

sections.h2: Door Frame Repair in Metro Detroit
body: Sometimes the door is fine and the frame is the real problem. Door frame repair is one of the jobs Metro Detroit homeowners call us for most, because Michigan weather is hard on jambs: water wicks up into the bottom of the frame and rots the wood, freeze-thaw movement opens the joints, and a single attempted break-in can split a jamb from the lock to the floor.
Watch for these signs that the frame, not the door, needs attention:

Our repair process starts with finding out how deep the damage goes. Surface splits are glued, clamped, and refastened into the framing. Rotted sections are cut out and rebuilt with new jamb stock, and kicked-in frames get reinforced strike plates with long screws that anchor into the stud behind the jamb. Before the repair, you have a frame that leaks air and can't hold a lock; after, the door closes flush against solid wood, latches cleanly, and seals tight. For a full breakdown of jamb rebuilds and security reinforcement, see our dedicated door frame & jamb repair service.
Working on a patio slider instead? Tracks, rollers, and sealed glass are their own specialty, covered on our sliding glass door repair page.

Is the garage the real headache? If the overhead door hums, grinds, or ignores the remote, the opener is usually to blame, and our garage door opener repair service covers every drive type across Metro Detroit.
bullets: Cracked or split wood around the strike plate or hinges
Soft, flaking, or discolored wood at the bottom of the jamb
A gap opening up between the frame and the wall or brickmould
Screws that keep pulling loose no matter how often you tighten them
A door that still won't latch after alignment and hinge adjustments

whats_included: Full diagnosis of why the door sticks, drags, or won't latch

faqs.q: Can you fix a door that won't close or latch, or do I need a new one?

faqs.q: My door only sticks in certain seasons. Is that worth fixing?

services.meta_title: Sliding Glass Door Repair Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Sliding glass door stuck, off-track, or drafty? BH Door Solutions handles roller, track & glass repair across Metro Detroit. Free estimates—call today.
services.h1: Sliding Glass Door Repair in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: Rollers, tracks, glass, and locks—we get your slider gliding smooth and sealing tight again.
services.intro_lead: When your sliding glass door drags, sticks, or won't lock, everyday life gets frustrating fast—and a drafty slider quietly drives up your heating bill all Michigan winter. BH Door Solutions handles sliding glass door repair across Metro Detroit, from worn rollers and bent tracks to foggy glass panels. One call gets your door gliding, sealing, and locking again.

body: Most sliding door trouble starts small—a little drag, a click that won't catch—and gets worse until the door barely moves. Our sliding glass door repair covers the full range of issues on residential patio doors and commercial storefront sliders alike, usually in a single visit with parts already on the truck.
Whatever your door is doing, we've fixed it before. Here's what we most often see and repair:
bullets: Worn or broken rollers that make the door drag, grind, and stick
Bent, dented, or dirty tracks that throw the door off its path
Off-track doors that have jumped the rail and won't slide at all
Sliders that won't lock, latch, or close flush against the frame
Foggy, cracked, or shattered glass panels

body: The rollers are the small wheels at the bottom of your door that carry all its weight, and they take a beating. Grit works into the wheels, the bearings wear out, and the door starts to grind against the frame. We swap worn roller assemblies for heavy-duty replacements and adjust their height so the door hangs level and rolls smooth.

The track matters just as much. A track that's bent, packed with debris, or corroded will chew through brand-new rollers in months. Our roller & track repair includes straightening the track, clearing it out, and lubricating it—and if a door has jumped off the rail entirely, we re-seat it and correct whatever knocked it off in the first place.

When you see fog, haze, or moisture trapped between the panes, the sealed glass unit has failed. That's more than an eyesore—it means the insulating gas is gone and you're losing heat straight through the door. We handle sliding door glass replacement by swapping the failed insulated glass unit while keeping your existing frame, which restores clarity and stops the energy loss.

Cracked or shattered panels—from a stray ball, a storm, or an attempted break-in—get replaced the same way. We measure, source the right glass for your specific door, and install it so the panel is clear, solid, and sealed tight again.

sections.h2: Sliders That Won't Lock or Close
body: A sliding door that won't lock is both a security worry and a draft problem. Usually the culprit is a worn latch, a misaligned strike, or a door sitting too low on failed rollers to line up with the lock. We repair or replace the lock hardware and adjust the door until it closes flush and latches with an easy, solid catch.

If your door was forced or damaged in a break-in attempt or a storm, this is exactly the kind of urgent repair we tackle promptly—often same-day when the schedule allows during business hours—so your home is buttoned up and secure again.

Michigan's freeze-thaw cycle is brutal on sliding doors. Water gets into the track, freezes, and warps the aluminum; cold turns worn weatherstripping brittle; and a slider that won't seal lets winter drafts pour in. We fix the mechanical problem and reseal the door so it holds up through the next season—not just until the next cold snap.

Every sliding glass door repair starts with a free, no-obligation estimate and honest flat-rate pricing, so there are no surprises. We're licensed and insured, we back our work with a workmanship guarantee, and because our trucks come fully stocked, most repairs are finished in one visit. Same-day service is available when the schedule allows.

sections.h2: Sliding Door Repair Across Metro Detroit's Suburbs
body: Sliding doors age differently depending on where you live and when your home was built, and we work on the full range across Metro Detroit every week. In the postwar brick ranches of Warren and St. Clair Shores, we often find original aluminum-frame sliders whose shallow tracks have worn thin and whose small steel rollers were never built for decades of daily use. The colonials and split-levels that filled Troy, Rochester Hills, and Farmington Hills from the 1970s onward tend to carry heavier wood and clad-wood patio doors, where humid Michigan summers swell the stiles and the sill until the panel drags. Newer subdivisions in Novi and Canton mostly run vinyl sliders, which hold their shape well but ride on plastic tandem rollers that flatten and crack as they age.

The track type matters as much as the door. Worn aluminum tracks can often be revived with a stainless track cap instead of a full track replacement, which saves tearing out the frame. Bent or dented tracks get straightened and cleaned, and corroded rollers are swapped for sealed, height-adjustable assemblies matched to your door's weight, whether that's a light single-pane slider or a heavy insulated glass panel.

We also handle the details that make a slider livable: sticky screen doors that derail in the wind, handles and mortise-style latches that seize up in the cold, and weatherstripping that lets a winter draft pour through the meeting rail. For businesses, we service heavier commercial sliders and multi-panel systems with the same stocked-truck, one-visit approach.

Glass is its own story. Close to the water in St. Clair Shores and Grosse Pointe Woods, constant lake humidity is tough on sealed units, and fogged or hazy panels are one of the most common calls we get there. We replace the insulated glass unit while keeping your existing frame, which restores the view and the insulation without the price of a new door. The same goes for panels cracked by a storm or a stray baseball anywhere from Royal Oak to Ferndale, including the narrower sliders common in condos and townhouses there.

Wherever you are within about 35 miles of Detroit, across Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb counties, the visit works the same way: we diagnose the rollers, track, glass, and lock together, quote you a flat rate up front, and finish most sliding door repairs in a single trip because the parts are already on the truck. Same-day service is available across the suburbs when the schedule allows, so call early if your slider has jumped the track or won't lock tonight.

whats_included: A full inspection of the rollers, track, glass, lock, and weatherstripping
Worn roller assemblies swapped for heavy-duty replacements
Track straightening, cleaning, and lubrication
Re-seating off-track panels back onto the rail
Lock and latch repair or replacement, adjusted to close flush
Foggy, cracked, or shattered glass panel replacement
Fresh weatherstripping to stop drafts and air leaks
Cleanup and a smooth-glide test before we leave

why_us.text: We roll up with rollers, tracks, lock hardware, and weatherstripping already on board, so most sliding door repairs are done in a single visit—no waiting around for a second trip.

faqs.q: Can you repair my sliding door, or do I need a whole new one?
faqs.a: In most cases we can repair it, not replace it. Worn rollers, bent tracks, failed locks, and off-track panels are all fixable with parts we stock on our trucks. A full door replacement is usually only necessary when the frame itself is damaged or rotted. We'll tell you honestly during your free estimate.

faqs.q: Why is my sliding glass door so hard to open?
faqs.a: A slider that drags or sticks almost always comes down to worn rollers or a dirty, bent track. Dirt, grit, and Michigan freeze-thaw grind down the roller wheels and dent the aluminum track over time. We replace the rollers, straighten and clean the track, and adjust the height so it glides freely again.

faqs.q: Can you replace just the foggy glass panel?
faqs.a: Yes. When you see fog or moisture between the panes, the glass seal has failed—but the frame is usually still fine. We replace the insulated glass unit itself rather than the whole door, which restores clarity and stops the energy loss. Cracked or shattered panels get swapped the same way.

faqs.q: How much does sliding glass door repair cost?
faqs.a: Most sliding door repairs are affordable and far cheaper than replacing the whole door. Typical roller and track repairs run in the low-to-mid hundreds, while glass panel replacement depends on size and pane type. Your exact price depends on the parts and door, so we give you a free, no-obligation estimate upfront with flat-rate pricing.

services.meta_description: Need door frame repair in Metro Detroit? BH fixes split, rotted, kicked-in frames and jambs, often same-day. Free estimates, flat-rate pricing. Call today.
services.h1: Door Frame Repair in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: Split, rotted, or kicked in? We rebuild door frames and jambs solid and weathertight, with same-day service across Metro Detroit when the schedule allows.
services.intro_lead: The frame and jamb are what hold your door square, sealed, and locked, so when they split, rot, or get kicked in, the whole opening stops doing its job. If you need door frame repair in Metro Detroit, BH Door Solutions rebuilds the frame and jamb so the door closes tight, latches clean, and keeps Michigan weather where it belongs, outside. We diagnose the real cause, give you an honest flat-rate price, and fix it in one visit whenever the parts and access allow.

sections.h2: Split, Rotted & Warped Frames and Jambs
body: Wood frames and jambs take a beating in Michigan. Years of freeze-thaw, wind-driven rain, and snowmelt wicking up from the threshold cause the bottom of a jamb to soften, split, and rot, often hidden behind the weatherstripping and trim where you can't see it until the door starts sagging or the deadbolt won't line up.

Not every soft spot means a full teardown. Where the damage is limited we cut out the rotted section, splice in new solid or composite jamb stock, and prime and seal it so water can't get back in. When the split runs the length of the jamb or the frame has warped out of square, a full door frame repair or replacement is the honest call, and we'll show you which one you're actually looking at.
bullets: Soft, spongy, or crumbling wood at the base of the jamb
Cracks or splits running down the strike or hinge side
A frame bowed out of square so the door won't sit flush
Peeling paint, dark staining, or a musty smell near the threshold

body: When a frame is too far gone to patch, replacing it is the fix that lasts. We remove the old frame and jamb, check the rough opening and subfloor for hidden water damage, and set a new frame plumb, level, and square, the three things that decide whether a door swings and latches right for years.

We match your existing setup, from standard residential prehung openings to heavier commercial steel frames on high-traffic entrances. Every replacement gets properly shimmed, fastened into the framing studs, insulated at the gaps, and sealed, so you're not just getting a new frame, you're getting a door that opens easy, closes tight, and locks securely.

A forced entry almost never breaks the lock, it splinters the jamb where the strike plate is screwed in, which makes kick-in damage one of the more urgent kinds of door frame repair we handle. After a break-in you're often left with a working deadbolt but a shattered jamb that won't hold it, so we treat these calls as a priority and get out same-day when possible during business hours to secure the opening.

We replace the split jamb section, reinforce the strike area with a heavy-duty strike plate and long screws that anchor into the wall framing instead of just the trim, and re-hang the door so it closes and locks like new. Where you want extra peace of mind, we can add a reinforced strike box or security jamb hardware so the next attempt has a lot more to get through.
bullets: Splintered jamb around the strike plate or deadbolt
Door that latches but no longer feels secure
Reinforced strike plates and longer framing screws
Optional security jamb hardware for added protection

body: If you feel a draft or see daylight around a closed door, the seal between the door and the frame has failed, and in a Michigan winter that gap is pouring heat straight out of your home. Weatherstripping & draft sealing is the quiet part of frame work that makes the biggest difference to your comfort and your energy bills.

We replace worn or compressed weatherstripping in the jamb, adjust the door so it meets the seal evenly all the way around, and close up the gaps that let in cold air, water, and street noise. The result is a door that shuts with a solid, quiet thud instead of a rattle, and a home that holds its heat where you paid to put it.

The threshold and door sweep are the seal along the bottom of the opening, and they're the first parts to wear out because they take every footstep, every snow boot, and every puddle. A worn sweep or a warped, pitted threshold lets cold air, water, and even pests slip in under the door, and it's a common hidden cause of a rotting jamb.

We replace door sweeps, reset or swap adjustable thresholds, and re-establish a clean seal at the sill so water drains away from the frame instead of into it. Weatherization is the part of door frame repair that keeps the frame dry, so we seal the whole opening, top, sides, and bottom, as one system that lasts.
bullets: Worn, torn, or dragging door sweeps replaced
Adjustable thresholds reset for a tight bottom seal
Sill sealed so water drains away from the frame
Whole-opening weatherization, top to bottom

whats_included: On-site diagnosis of what's causing the frame or jamb to fail
Rot and split repair, splicing in new solid or composite jamb stock
Full frame and jamb replacement set plumb, level, and square
Kick-in and break-in jamb repair with reinforced strike plates and framing screws
Fresh weatherstripping and even draft sealing around the whole opening
New door sweeps and adjustable thresholds reset for a tight bottom seal
Priming and sealing so the repair stands up to Michigan weather
Upfront flat-rate price and a workmanship guarantee

why_us.title: We fix the frame, not just the door
why_us.text: Plenty of companies will re-hang a door on a rotted or split jamb and call it done. We repair the frame and jamb underneath so the fix actually holds, your door latches securely, and the same problem doesn't come back next season.

why_us.title: One-visit repairs, fully stocked trucks
why_us.text: Our trucks carry jamb stock, weatherstripping, thresholds, sweeps, strike plates, and framing screws, so most frame and jamb repairs are finished in a single visit instead of a wait-for-parts return trip, with same-day service available when the schedule allows.

why_us.title: Local crews who know Michigan frames
why_us.text: We repair frames across Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb every week and know exactly how freeze-thaw, snowmelt, and winter drafts destroy a jamb, so we seal and rebuild for the climate, not just for today.

faqs.q: Can you repair just the frame, or do I have to replace the whole door?
faqs.a: In most cases we can repair the frame and jamb without touching the door itself. If the damage is limited rot, a split, or a kicked-in strike area, we splice in new jamb stock and reinforce it, saving you the cost of a full replacement. We only recommend replacing the frame when it's warped badly out of square or the rot has spread too far, and we'll show you which you're facing.

faqs.q: My door frame is rotting at the bottom. What causes that in Michigan?
faqs.a: Bottom-of-jamb rot is caused by water, and Michigan gives it plenty of chances. Snowmelt, wind-driven rain, and freeze-thaw let moisture wick up into the wood behind the weatherstripping and threshold, where it sits and slowly softens the jamb. We cut out the rot, splice in sealed replacement stock, and correct the drainage and seal so it doesn't creep back.

faqs.q: Someone kicked in my door. Can you repair the jamb?
faqs.a: Yes, jamb repair after a break-in is one of the most common repairs we do. A forced entry usually splinters the jamb around the strike plate rather than breaking the lock, so we replace the damaged section and reinforce the strike area with heavy-duty plates and long screws anchored into the framing. We treat these as a priority and get out same-day when possible during business hours.

faqs.q: Will fixing the frame stop the drafts around my door?
faqs.a: Yes, sealing the frame is exactly how we stop door drafts. Most cold air sneaks in through failed weatherstripping in the jamb, a worn sweep, or a gap at the threshold, not through the door slab itself. Our weatherstripping and draft sealing resets that seal all the way around the opening, so the door shuts tight and your heat stays inside where it belongs.

services.meta_title: Door Lock & Hardware Repair Metro Detroit | BH Door Solutions
services.meta_description: Door lock and hardware repair across Metro Detroit — deadbolts, rekeying, smart locks, handlesets and closers, done right. Free estimates, call today.
services.h1: Door Lock and Hardware Repair & Smart Lock Installation in Metro Detroit
services.hero_tagline: Deadbolts that won't throw, wobbly handles, a lock that won't turn in the cold — we install, rekey, and repair door hardware right the first time, often the same day.
services.intro_lead: A deadbolt that sticks, a handle that spins loose in your hand, or a lock that won't catch when the temperature drops is more than an annoyance — it's your home's security on the line. BH Door Solutions handles door lock and hardware repair across Metro Detroit, from a single stubborn deadbolt to a full smart lock installation, rekeying every door to one key, or swapping tired handlesets for something that looks and feels solid. You get honest flat-rate pricing, a one-visit fix, and hardware that lines up and locks clean through every Michigan season.

body: A deadbolt is only as strong as how it's mounted. We install ANSI Grade 1 and Grade 2 deadbolts with a full one-inch throw bolt, then back them with a reinforced strike plate and three-inch screws that bite into the wall framing instead of just the jamb — the single biggest difference between a lock that deters a kick-in and one that doesn't. If your deadbolt catches, won't throw, or only locks when you lift or shove the door, the cause is usually seasonal movement: Michigan's freeze-thaw cycle shifts the door in its frame until the bolt no longer lines up with the strike. We realign the strike and latch so it throws with one easy turn.

Rekeying is the fast, affordable answer when you've just moved in, lost a key, or handed spares to a contractor you'd rather not have holding one. Instead of buying all-new locks, we change the pins inside your existing cylinders so every old key stops working — and we can key-alike your front, side, and garage-entry doors so a single key runs the whole house. Sticking cylinders, worn keys, and locks that grind are all part of the same door lock and hardware repair visit.

A smart lock installation ends the hunt for keys for good — punch in a code, tap your phone, or let it lock itself behind you. We install keypad, Bluetooth, and Wi-Fi models from the brands homeowners actually trust, set up guest codes for family, dog walkers, and cleaners, and dial in auto-lock so the door never gets left unlocked by accident. Most quality smart locks keep a traditional keyed backup, so you're never stranded if the batteries die.

Michigan winters are hard on smart hardware: cold drains batteries faster and a door that has shifted in the cold makes the motor fight a misaligned bolt until it jams. That's why we don't just bolt one on — we match the lock to your door's thickness and backset, correct the strike alignment first, and test that the bolt drives home effortlessly before we hand it back to you. Retrofit onto your existing deadbolt or a full replacement, we set it up so it works every time.

The handle is what your hand touches a dozen times a day, so a loose, wobbly, or tarnished one drags down the whole entrance. We handle handleset and lever replacement across the board — grip-style entry sets for the front door, passage and privacy knobs for interior rooms, dummy hardware for closets and French doors, and ADA-friendly levers that are easier on arthritic hands and required in most commercial settings. We match the finish across your doors so nothing looks mismatched, and re-anchor everything to sit tight instead of working loose again in a month. This is the kind of door lock and hardware repair that quietly makes a home feel finished.

Multi-point locks are the tall locking systems on many fiberglass entry doors and patio doors — lift the handle and the door bolts at the top, middle, and bottom at once for a weather-tight, secure seal. When the gearbox wears out or the lift handle goes limp, the whole door stops locking. We repair and replace the gearbox, tie bars, and keeps, and realign the strikes so all three points engage smoothly again.

For businesses, the door closer is what keeps a storefront or office entrance from slamming, standing open, or dragging in a draft. We install and fine-tune hydraulic closers — adjusting sweep speed, latch speed, and backcheck, and setting the closing force to meet ADA requirements so the door is easy to open but always pulls itself shut and latched. From high-traffic entrances to interior fire-rated doors, we keep them cycling reliably.

sections.h2: Hinges, Strike Plates & Glass Insert Replacement
body: A lot of lock trouble is really hardware trouble one hinge away. Stripped hinge screws let a door sag until the deadbolt misses the strike entirely, so we re-anchor them with longer fasteners into the stud or swap in ball-bearing hinges for heavy and high-traffic doors. We also upgrade thin builder-grade strike plates to reinforced, four-screw security strikes that spread a forced blow across the framing — a small, inexpensive part that makes a real difference at your most-used door. If you've searched for door lock and hardware repair because the door itself has drifted out of square, this is usually where the fix starts.

We also replace the decorative and privacy glass inserts set into many entry and storm doors. When an insert fogs up because its seal has failed, cracks from a stray ball, or you simply want more light or more privacy, we measure the opening, source a matching snap-in insert, and swap it cleanly without replacing the whole door.

whats_included: Free on-site check of your locks, keys, hardware, and door alignment
New deadbolt, handleset, lever, or smart lock supplied and installed — your choice of style and finish
Rekeying of existing cylinders and fresh keys cut, keyed-alike on request
Reinforced strike plate anchored with three-inch screws into the framing
Strike and latch realignment so the bolt throws and locks smoothly
Smart lock setup: mounting, app pairing, guest codes, and auto-lock
Multi-point lock and door-closer repair, replacement, and adjustment
Hinge, strike-plate, and glass-insert repair, plus a full function test and cleanup

why_us.title: Security-Grade Installs, Not Just a Swap
why_us.text: We fit ANSI-graded deadbolts, reinforced strike plates, and three-inch screws driven into the wall framing — the details that actually stop a forced entry. Anyone can screw on a knob; we install hardware that holds when it counts.

why_us.title: We Fix the Door Behind the Lock
why_us.text: Most locks that won't catch aren't broken — the door has shifted with Michigan's freeze-thaw seasons and the bolt no longer meets the strike. We realign the door, hinges, and strike so the fix actually lasts, on both homes and commercial entrances, with one call.

why_us.title: One Visit, Honest Flat-Rate Pricing
why_us.text: Our trucks carry the deadbolts, cylinders, handlesets, closers, and smart locks to finish most jobs in a single visit. You get a free estimate and an upfront flat rate before we start — licensed, insured, and backed by our workmanship guarantee.

faqs.q: My deadbolt only locks if I lift or push the door — what's wrong?
faqs.a: The lock is fine; the door has shifted so the bolt no longer lines up with the strike. Michigan's freeze-thaw swings move doors in their frames, and loose hinges make it worse. We realign the door, tighten or shim the hinges, and reposition the strike plate so the deadbolt throws smoothly with one easy turn — usually in a single visit, no new lock required.

faqs.q: Can you rekey my locks instead of replacing them?
faqs.a: Yes, and it's usually the smarter spend. Rekeying changes the pins inside your existing cylinders so every old key stops working — ideal after a move, a lost key, or handing spares to a contractor. We can also key-alike your entry, side, and garage doors so one key opens them all. You keep your current hardware and pay a fraction of full replacement.

faqs.q: Do smart locks hold up in Michigan winters?
faqs.a: Yes, quality smart locks handle Michigan cold well — the main effect is that batteries drain faster, so we recommend fresh lithium cells and check them at install. We also correct the strike alignment first so the motor isn't fighting a shifted bolt, which is the usual cause of a jamming smart lock. Most models keep a keyed backup, so you're never locked out.

faqs.q: Do you install locks and hardware for businesses too?
faqs.a: Yes, we serve both homes and businesses across Metro Detroit. For commercial doors that means ADA-compliant levers, hydraulic door closers adjusted to code, panic and exit hardware, and heavy-duty deadbolts and strikes built for constant daily use. One call covers your storefront, office, and back entrances, with the same flat-rate pricing and one-visit service.

sections.h2: Panic Bars, Exit Devices & Commercial Hardware
body: Panic bars and exit devices aren't optional. Fire and building codes require them on most commercial egress doors, and when one sticks, dogs open when it shouldn't, or won't latch, you have both a security gap and a compliance problem. We repair, adjust, and replace rim, mortise, and vertical-rod panic bars & exit devices, including electrified hardware tied to access control.

We also service the hardware that makes a commercial door work every day: door closers that drift or slam, often from cold-thickened fluid in winter; mortise and cylindrical locksets; continuous and heavy-duty hinges; and weatherstripping that's let the drafts in. Dialing in the closer sweep and latch timing keeps the door protecting itself for years.

services.intro_lead: When you tap the remote and nothing happens, or the motor hums while the door stays put, the problem usually lives in the opener, not the door. BH Door Solutions repairs garage door openers for homes and businesses across Metro Detroit, from Detroit and Dearborn to Royal Oak, Troy, and Sterling Heights. sections.h2: Signs your garage door opener needs repair
body: Openers rarely quit without warning. The symptoms below are the ones Metro Detroit homeowners call us about most, and each one points to a specific, fixable fault.

None of these mean you automatically need a new opener. A stripped gear, a worn trolley, a misaligned safety sensor, or a remote that needs reprogramming are all standard repairs we complete in one visit.
bullets: The door doesn't respond to the remote or wall button, or only works from a few feet away
The motor runs with a grinding or rattling noise but the door barely moves
Remotes or the keypad lose their programming, work intermittently, or lose range
The door starts to close, then reverses before it reaches the floor
The trolley slides along the rail but the door itself stays shut
The opener light blinks and the motor clicks without lifting the door

sections.h2: Chain, belt, screw, and direct drive: we repair all four
body: Every residential opener moves the door one of four ways, and each drive type wears in its own pattern. Knowing which one hangs in your garage tells us a lot before we even arrive.

Chain drive openers are the workhorses of Metro Detroit garages: affordable, durable, and a little noisy. Their weak points are chain slack, worn sprockets, and stripped drive gears, all of which we adjust or replace. Belt drive units run quietly, which makes them popular under bedrooms, but the belt itself can fray, glaze, or lose tension over years of cycles. Screw drive openers move the door with a long threaded rod and have few moving parts, but they are sensitive to Michigan's temperature swings and depend on fresh lubrication to keep from binding in a cold snap. Direct drive models move the motor itself along a stationary chain, so there is very little to wear, though sensors, remotes, and travel settings still fail the same way they do on every other type.
Whichever type you own, the diagnosis follows the same path: we test the motor, the drive, the safety sensors, and the controls, then fix the part that actually failed instead of guessing.

sections.h2: Repair or replace? An honest answer
body: Most opener problems end in a repair, not a replacement. Stripped gears, worn trolleys, frayed belts, misaligned photo eyes, faulty wall buttons, and remotes that lost their code are all fixable for far less than the cost of a new unit.

We recommend replacement in a few specific situations: the motor itself has burned out, the same unit keeps failing repair after repair, parts for a discontinued model are no longer available, or an older opener lacks working safety reversal. If replacement really is the smarter spend, we'll say so plainly, walk you through quiet belt drive and heavy-duty chain drive options, and back the installation with the same workmanship guarantee as every repair. It's the same straight repair-versus-replace talk we bring to door repair throughout the house.

sections.h2: Common garage door opener repairs we handle
body: And if the diagnosis shows the door itself is the real culprit, a bind in the tracks or a door dragging out of balance, we handle that side too, along with locks and hardware on the rest of your entries.

why_us.text: Drive gears, trolleys, safety sensors, remotes, and common hardware ride on the truck, so most opener repairs are finished in a single visit instead of a wait-for-parts return trip.

why_us.text: A door that won't move can be the motor, the drive, a sensor, or the door itself. We test each one and repair the part that actually failed, so the fix holds instead of failing again next month.

why_us.title: Local specialists who know Michigan garages
why_us.text: Cold snaps stiffen lubricant, swell doors, and expose weak gears every winter. We repair openers across Wayne, Oakland, and Macomb every week and fix for the season, not just the moment.

faqs.q: My garage door opener won't respond to the remote or wall button. What should I check first?
faqs.a: Start with the simple things: a dead remote battery, the lock or vacation switch on the wall console, which disables remotes when it is on, and the outlet the opener plugs into. If those all check out and the door still won't respond, the fault is usually in the receiver board, the wall-button wiring, or the remotes themselves. We test each link in that chain and repair the one that failed.

faqs.q: Why is my opener grinding but the garage door won't move?
faqs.a: A grinding or rattling motor with a door that stays shut usually means the main drive gear has stripped or the trolley that pulls the door has worn out or disengaged. Stop running the opener once you hear grinding, because every cycle chews the gear further. Both repairs are jobs we can usually finish in a single visit with parts from the truck.

faqs.q: Why does my garage door start to close and then reverse back up?
faqs.a: That reversal is the opener's safety system doing its job, just too eagerly. The photo-eye sensors near the floor may be misaligned, blocked, or wired loose, or the travel and force settings may need adjustment. We realign the sensors, check the wiring, and reset the limits so the door closes fully and still reverses when something is actually in its path.

faqs.q: Should I repair my garage door opener or replace it?
faqs.a: If the fix is a gear, a sensor, a remote, or an adjustment, repair is almost always the smarter spend. We lean toward replacement when the motor itself has failed, when the same unit keeps breaking down, or when an older opener lacks working safety reversal, since parts for discontinued models can be hard to source. Either way you get an honest recommendation and a flat-rate price before any work begins.

faqs.q: What kind of opener do I have: chain, belt, screw, or direct drive?
faqs.a: Look at the rail above your parked car. A metal chain like a bicycle's means chain drive, a smooth rubber belt means belt drive, and a long threaded steel rod means screw drive. If the motor itself travels along the rail instead of sitting in one spot, it is a direct drive. We repair all four types across Metro Detroit.

faqs.q: Do you offer same-day garage door opener repair near me?
faqs.a: Same-day service is often available across Metro Detroit during our business hours, Sunday through Thursday 9am to 5pm and Friday 9am to noon. We reach Detroit, Royal Oak, Troy, Warren, Sterling Heights, and the rest of our service area from local trucks, so call early in the day for the best shot at a same-day slot.
